name: VOD HTTP Filesystem
description: Expose your Dispatcharr VOD library to Plex/Jellyfin as a mountable HTTP filesystem with Plex-correct naming, category support, multi-provider streaming, and accurate seekable playback via Dispatcharr's native VOD proxy
author: onehottake

fields.label: 📋 Getting started
fields.description: 1) Click 🚀 Enable. 2) Click 📋 rclone Config and follow it (it links to /vodfs/rclone_conf on this host). 3) Point Plex at /mnt/vodfs/Movies/<Category> (per-category, not /All). 4) Click 💧 Hydrate Now — titles appear as Dispatcharr fetches their size. 5) Click 🩺 Status anytime to see progress. Tip: open /vodfs/ in a browser to inspect the filesystem.

fields.label: HTTP Port
fields.help_text: Container-internal port for the HTTP filesystem server. Served to rclone/Plex via Dispatcharr's nginx at /vodfs/.

fields.label: Dispatcharr Base URL
fields.help_text: Internal Dispatcharr URL the plugin uses to build 302 redirect targets. The reachable rclone/Plex URL is derived from your browser request, not this.

fields.label: Enable Authentication (Token-based)
fields.help_text: Require a valid Dispatcharr API key (Authorization: ApiKey <key> or X-API-Key). Default OFF for easy browser inspection and setup.

fields.label: ⏰ Hydration (size backfill)
fields.description: vodfs only shows titles whose size Dispatcharr already knows (stored bitrate). Hydration fetches that over time — movie detail + un-hydrated series — paced so the provider is never hammered. It runs a full pass on enable (if enabled below), then at the scheduled times. Pin it off-peak (e.g. 3am) so it never runs while you're watching.

fields.label: ⏰ Scheduled Run Times (24-hour)
fields.help_text: Comma-separated HHMM times to run hydration (e.g. 0300 = 3am). Blank = never on a schedule (manual/on-load only).

fields.label: 🌍 Timezone
fields.help_text: IANA timezone for the scheduled times (e.g. America/Chicago, Europe/London).

fields.label: 🐢 Hydration Rate (per second)
fields.help_text: Refreshes submitted per second — the throttle. Higher = faster but more provider load. 0 disables hydration entirely.

fields.label: Full sync on enable
fields.help_text: Run a full hydration pass when the server starts, then on the schedule above.

actions.label: Show rclone Configuration
actions.description: Where to get the copy/paste rclone config (the /vodfs/rclone_conf page on this host)

actions.label: Hydrate Now
actions.description: Immediately run a hydration pass (backfill movie/series sizes), paced by the rate limit

actions.label: Status
actions.description: Server health, how many titles are visible vs pending hydration, and the next scheduled run

confirm.message: Are you sure you want to stop the HTTP filesystem server?
